issue.title: RunPod Network Volume → local mlruns sync

agent.name: dtype-safety
agent.focus: BF16/FP16 dtype correctness, torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported() usage, no hardcoded torch.float16

agent.name: cloud-config
agent.focus: Zero hardcoded cloud/GPU/registry values, all config via Hydra groups

agent.name: test-coverage
agent.focus: No ghost completions, all assertions meaningful, proper pytest markers

metadata.context: SAM3 NaN investigation H1 CONFIRMED: val_loss=NaN was a sentinel (validation never ran). Run 2 (AMP OFF) produced all-finite metrics: val_loss=0.7254, train_loss=0.725, val_dice=0.301. Run 2 was 3/5 epochs at last check (2026-03-15 03:20 UTC). Should be finished. Next: Phase 3 (re-enable AMP) to confirm AMP is the root cause, then BF16 dtype fix.

phase.name: Collect Run 2 Results and Pre-flight
phase.description: Collect Run 2 results from cloud MLflow (sam3_hybrid, AMP OFF, RTX 4090). Run 2 started at 02:14 UTC, 22 min/epoch × 5 epochs = ~110 min. Expected completion: ~04:04 UTC. Should be done by now. Update audit trail in sam3-nan-loss-fix.md with final metrics.

task.name: Collect Run 2 final metrics from cloud MLflow
task.description: Query cloud MLflow for the sam3_hybrid_cloud Run 2 results. Expected: 5 epochs all finite, val_loss decreasing, val_dice improving. Update docs/planning/sam3-nan-loss-fix.md audit trail table.

tdd-spec.red: No tests — this is data collection and documentation update.
tdd-spec.green: Query MLflow via ralph_monitor or direct API. Record: final train_loss, val_loss, val_dice for all 5 epochs. Update Run 2 row in audit trail. Add Run 3 planned row.

task.acceptance: Run 2 audit trail row has all 5 epoch metrics. Run 3 row exists as planned with AMP ON config.

task.name: Verify cloud credentials and RunPod balance
task.description: Pre-flight check before launching Run 3: - sky check runpod → enabled - RunPod API balance check (user has $11.40) - MLflow health check - Docker image exists in registry

tdd-spec.red: No tests — pre-flight validation script already exists.

task.acceptance: All pre-flight checks pass. RunPod balance sufficient for 3+ runs.

phase.name: SAM3 BF16 Dtype Auto-Detection
phase.description: Implement auto-detection of BF16 support per the feedback memory and metalearning doc. SAM3 HuggingFace uses torch.bfloat16; our code hardcodes torch.float16 (written for RTX 2070 Super / Turing which lacks BF16). On Ampere+ GPUs (A100, RTX 3090/4090), BF16 is strictly better: same exponent range as FP32, no 65504 overflow risk. This is issue #712.

task.name: Write BF16 auto-detection tests
task.description: Test that sam3_backbone.py selects BF16 on Ampere+ GPUs and falls back to FP16 on Turing. Mock torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported() for both paths.

tdd-spec.red: test_bf16_selected_when_supported: mock is_bf16_supported()=True → torch.bfloat16 test_fp16_fallback_when_bf16_unsupported: mock is_bf16_supported()=False → torch.float16 test_dtype_logged_to_mlflow: verify dtype choice is logged as a param test_encoder_output_dtype_matches: encoder output dtype matches selected dtype
tdd-spec.green: Add _select_encoder_dtype() function to sam3_backbone.py. Replace hardcoded torch.float16 with _select_encoder_dtype() call. Log selected dtype. Apply to Sam3Model.from_pretrained(torch_dtype=...).

task.acceptance: sam3_backbone.py uses torch.bfloat16 when torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported() is True. FP16 is fallback only. Dtype selection logged to MLflow. All 4 tests pass. Pre-commit hooks pass.

issue.reason: BF16 auto-detection implemented with Turing fallback

task.name: Update smoke test configs for BF16
task.description: Update cloud smoke test configs to explicitly set encoder_dtype: auto (default). Add encoder_dtype field to model_profiles YAML for documentation. Update adapters/CLAUDE.md TODO to mark as DONE.

tdd-spec.red: No new tests — config + documentation updates.
tdd-spec.green: Add encoder_dtype: auto to smoke configs. Update CLAUDE.md BF16 section: mark TODO as DONE, add verification date.

task.acceptance: CLAUDE.md BF16 TODO marked as DONE with implementation date. Config files reference encoder_dtype: auto.

phase.name: AMP Isolation — Run 3 on RunPod
phase.description: Re-enable AMP (mixed_precision: true) with the BF16 dtype fix from P1. If val_loss is finite → AMP works with BF16 encoder (FP16 was the problem). If val_loss is NaN → AMP + 3D operations is the root cause (MONAI #4243). This is the systematic Phase 3 from sam3-nan-loss-fix.md. Use ralph-loop for autonomous monitoring.

task.name: Create AMP+BF16 smoke test config
task.description: Create smoke_sam3_hybrid_amp_bf16.yaml: same as smoke_sam3_hybrid_cloud.yaml but with mixed_precision: true. The BF16 dtype fix from T1.1 will be active. This is Run 3 in the audit trail.

tdd-spec.green: Copy smoke_sam3_hybrid_cloud.yaml → smoke_sam3_hybrid_amp_bf16.yaml. Change mixed_precision: true. Add comment explaining Phase 3 test. Keep val_interval: 1, max_epochs: 5.

task.acceptance: Config file exists with mixed_precision: true and clear comments.

task.name: Launch Run 3 via ralph-loop and monitor
task.description: Launch sam3_hybrid with AMP+BF16 on RunPod RTX 4090 using ralph-loop. Monitor: val_loss finite? NaN guard fires? VRAM usage? Expected: ~22 min/epoch × 5 = ~110 min, ~$1.25. Record results in audit trail.

tdd-spec.red: No unit tests — cloud execution + monitoring.
tdd-spec.green: Use ralph-loop: /ralph-loop --model sam3_hybrid --cloud runpod Monitor every 30s. On completion, record all 5 epoch metrics. Update Run 3 row in audit trail table. Decision tree: val_loss finite → AMP works with BF16 → close investigation val_loss NaN → AMP + 3D is root cause → proceed to T2.3

task.acceptance: Run 3 completed. Audit trail updated with all metrics. Root cause determination: AMP+BF16 works OR AMP+3D confirmed broken.

task.name: Implement AMP-off-for-validation if AMP fails
task.description: CONDITIONAL: Only if Run 3 shows val_loss=NaN (AMP + 3D confirmed broken). Implement separate AMP policy: AMP ON for training, AMP OFF for validation. This is Phase 5 Option A from sam3-nan-loss-fix.md.

tdd-spec.red: test_amp_disabled_during_validation: verify autocast disabled in validate_epoch test_amp_enabled_during_training: verify autocast enabled in train_epoch test_amp_policy_configurable: verify mixed_precision_val config key
tdd-spec.green: Add mixed_precision_val: bool to TrainingConfig (default: False for SAM3 models). In trainer.py validate_epoch(): use autocast(enabled=self.config.mixed_precision_val). In trainer.py train_epoch(): unchanged (uses self.config.mixed_precision).

task.acceptance: Validation runs without AMP even when training uses AMP. All tests pass. sam3_hybrid configs set mixed_precision_val: false.

phase.name: Dynamic GPU Cost Estimator
phase.description: Build a cost estimator that uses measured epoch times from MLflow to predict total job cost. This addresses #717 and feeds into the FinOps pipeline (#683). Inputs: epoch_time_seconds (from MLflow), gpu_hourly_rate (from model_profiles), max_epochs, num_folds. Output: estimated_total_cost, cost_per_epoch.

task.name: Add epoch-0 cost prediction to infrastructure_timing
task.description: LIBRARY-FIRST: infrastructure_timing.py already has compute_cost_analysis(), log_cost_analysis(), and get_hourly_rate_usd(). Do NOT create a new module. Add estimate_cost_from_first_epoch() to the existing module: after epoch 0 completes, extrapolate total cost from measured epoch time. Hook into trainer.py to call after epoch 0 and at training end.

tdd-spec.red: test_predict_cost_from_epoch_time: 22 min/epoch × 5 epochs × $0.69/hr = $1.27 test_predict_cost_with_folds: 22 min × 5 epochs × 3 folds × $0.69/hr = $3.80 test_predict_includes_setup_overhead: add 5 min setup overhead per job test_predict_zero_when_local: no cost for local runs (hourly_rate=0) test_actual_cost_at_end: uses real elapsed time, not prediction
tdd-spec.green: Add estimate_cost_from_first_epoch(epoch_seconds, max_epochs, num_folds, hourly_rate, setup_minutes=5) to infrastructure_timing.py. Add log_predicted_cost() that calls existing log_cost_analysis() pattern. Hook into trainer.py after epoch 0 and at training end.

task.acceptance: Cost prediction correct to 2 decimal places. Uses existing infrastructure_timing.py — no new module created. estimated_total_cost logged to MLflow after epoch 0. actual_total_cost logged at training completion. All 5 tests pass.

issue.reason: Dynamic cost prediction added to infrastructure_timing.py

phase.name: RunPod Network Volume → Local mlruns Sync
phase.description: Build a sync workflow to pull cloud MLflow run data (mlruns/) from RunPod Network Volume to local machine for analysis. This addresses #716. Uses rsync over SSH (SkyPilot provides SSH access to running clusters).

task.name: Implement mlruns sync script
task.description: Create a sync script that pulls mlruns/ from a running or stopped SkyPilot cluster to the local machine. Uses sky cluster ssh + rsync. Must handle: running cluster (direct rsync), stopped cluster (sky start first), network volume (mount path from SkyPilot YAML).

tdd-spec.red: test_sync_command_generation: verify rsync command is correct test_sync_excludes_large_artifacts: exclude .onnx, checkpoints by default test_sync_creates_local_dir: target dir created if missing test_sync_dry_run: --dry-run flag shows what would be transferred test_sync_incremental: only new/changed files transferred
tdd-spec.green: Create MLrunsSyncer class. sync(cluster_name, remote_mlruns_path, local_mlruns_path, dry_run=False). Uses subprocess to call rsync via sky cluster ssh. Exclude patterns: *.onnx, *.pth, *.ckpt (configurable).

task.acceptance: Sync command generates correct rsync invocation. Excludes large artifacts by default. Dry-run mode works without transferring data.

task.name: Add Makefile/Justfile sync targets + .env.example vars
task.description: Add make sync-mlruns and just sync-mlruns targets. Usage: make sync-mlruns CLUSTER=minivess-smoke-test Rule #22: Add SKYPILOT_CLUSTER and MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_ROOT to .env.example FIRST.

tdd-spec.red: No unit tests — Makefile targets + env var registration.
tdd-spec.green: Add to .env.example (Rule #22 — single source of truth): SKYPILOT_CLUSTER=minivess-smoke-test MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_ROOT=/checkpoints Add sync-mlruns target to both Makefile and justfile. Default CLUSTER from ${SKYPILOT_CLUSTER:-minivess-smoke-test}. Default remote path from ${MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_ROOT:-/checkpoints}.

task.acceptance: SKYPILOT_CLUSTER and MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_ROOT in .env.example. make sync-mlruns CLUSTER=name works. just sync-mlruns name works.

phase.name: SkyPilot Spot Checkpointing
phase.description: Implement SkyPilot MOUNT_CACHED for automatic checkpoint persistence across spot preemptions. When a spot instance is preempted, checkpoints on the mounted volume survive. On restart, training resumes from the latest checkpoint. This addresses #686.

task.name: Implement checkpoint resume logic
task.description: Add checkpoint discovery and resume logic to train_flow.py. On startup, check if a checkpoint exists at the mounted path. If yes, load it and resume training from that epoch. Integrates with the atomic checkpoint writes already in checkpoint_utils.py.

tdd-spec.red: test_resume_from_checkpoint: finds latest checkpoint, returns epoch number test_no_checkpoint_starts_fresh: no checkpoint → epoch 0 test_resume_loads_model_state: model weights restored from checkpoint test_resume_loads_optimizer_state: optimizer state restored test_resume_integrity_check: SHA256 verified before loading
tdd-spec.green: Add find_latest_checkpoint(checkpoint_dir) → (path, epoch) or None. Add resume_from_checkpoint(model, optimizer, checkpoint_path) → epoch. Verify SHA256 integrity before loading (uses existing atomic checkpoint infra). Integrate into train_flow.py training loop.

task.acceptance: Training resumes from latest valid checkpoint. Corrupted checkpoints (bad SHA256) are skipped with warning. Fresh start when no checkpoint exists.

issue.reason: Spot checkpoint resume implemented with SHA256 integrity

task.name: Add MOUNT_CACHED to all training SkyPilot YAMLs
task.description: Update ALL SkyPilot training YAMLs to use file_mounts with MOUNT_CACHED mode for the checkpoint directory. This persists checkpoints across preemptions. Applies to every YAML that runs training (including HPO).

tdd-spec.red: No unit tests — YAML config changes.
tdd-spec.green: Add file_mounts section with MOUNT_CACHED for /checkpoints. Add run: section logic to check for existing checkpoint on startup.

task.acceptance: SkyPilot YAML includes MOUNT_CACHED for checkpoint dir. run: script checks for existing checkpoint before training.

phase.name: Verification Run — Full Stack on RunPod
phase.description: Final verification run with all fixes integrated: - BF16 auto-detection (P1) - Cost estimation logged (P3) - Spot checkpoint resume (P5) Run sam3_hybrid with AMP ON + BF16 encoder on RunPod RTX 4090. Monitored via ralph-loop.

task.name: Launch verification run via ralph-loop
task.description: Launch sam3_hybrid with full stack on RunPod. Verify: BF16 dtype selected, cost estimate logged after epoch 0, checkpoint written atomically, val_loss finite.

tdd-spec.red: No unit tests — cloud verification via ralph-loop.
tdd-spec.green: /ralph-loop --model sam3_hybrid --cloud runpod Verify in MLflow: - sys_encoder_dtype = "torch.bfloat16" - estimated_total_cost param exists after epoch 0 - checkpoint files have .sha256 sidecar - val_loss finite for all epochs Update audit trail with Run 4 (or Run 3 if AMP test succeeded).

task.acceptance: All verification criteria pass. Audit trail complete. Investigation closed with confirmed root cause and fix.

close.reason: T1.1: BF16 auto-detection implemented with Turing fallback
T3.1: Dynamic GPU cost estimator implemented
T5.1: Spot checkpoint resume with SHA256 integrity

issue.title: P1: Separate AMP policy for train vs validation (conditional)
issue.condition: Only if Run 3 val_loss=NaN (AMP + 3D confirmed broken)

execution-notes.note: Session 1 (local): P0 + P1 + P3 + P4 + P5 (~14 TDD iterations)
Session 2 (cloud): P2 + P6 (~5 iterations + ralph-loop monitoring)
P2 is conditional: if Run 2 already proves AMP+FP16 works, skip to P6
T2.3 is conditional: only if Run 3 val_loss=NaN. If Run 3 succeeds, mark T2.3 NOT_APPLICABLE
Ralph-loop monitors cloud phases autonomously — user can be offline
All cloud execution uses Docker images via image_id: — no bare VM
BF16 fix (P1) should be committed and Docker image rebuilt before P2

risk.name: RunPod RTX 4090 sold out
risk.description: RTX 4090 may not be available in preferred region.
risk.mitigation: launch_smoke_test.py rotates through 17 regions. Fallback: A100 ($1.29/hr).

risk.name: BF16 causes different numerical behavior
risk.description: BF16 has lower mantissa precision than FP16 (7 vs 10 bits). Model may behave differently.
risk.mitigation: Compare Run 2 (FP16, AMP OFF) vs Run 4 (BF16). Metrics should be similar within 5%.

risk.name: AMP + BF16 still produces NaN
risk.description: Even with BF16 encoder, AMP autocast on 3D DynUNet may overflow.
risk.mitigation: T2.3 implements separate AMP policy (train=ON, val=OFF) as fallback.
